text: Ink is a smoke screen — and sometimes a decoy — for escape. Octopuses face predators like sharks and eels. Their ink gives them a fighting chance. When threatened, they release ink that clouds the water. This ink contains a chemical that can even dull a predator’s sense of smell. It's not just a getaway tactic; it can also confuse attackers, allowing for a clean escape. Want to dive deeper into ocean mysteries? Hit subscribe and explore with us!
